objective: Upgrade the segmentation backend registry to expose SAM 2.1 baseline, SAM 3.1 challenger, and manual reviewed masks. Verify SAM 3.1 exists in the official Meta (facebookresearch) repository; pin its source revision and checkpoint hash; the backend reports unavailable until pins are verified. Never install a CUDA environment into the MacBook base environment.
current_state: segmentation/ package has base.py, registry.py, manual_backend.py, sam2_backend.py, sam3_backend.py placeholder. Whether an official 'SAM 3.1' release exists is unverified.
assumptions: If no official Meta SAM 3.1 source revision + checkpoint can be verified, ship the unavailable-until-pinned pattern with the verification result documented in the backend docstring, and report it — do not substitute an unofficial fork or invent a pin.
acceptance_criteria: Registry lists sam2.1 (baseline), sam3.1 (challenger), manual; existing backend behavior unchanged.
sam3.1 backend refuses to run until a pinned official source revision and checkpoint hash verify; pins recorded in code as constants with provenance comments.
Backend identity (name, source revision, checkpoint hash) flows into segmentation provenance output.
No dependency additions to the base environment for CUDA; heavyweight deps remain optional/extra.
Segmentation remains a semantic-prior producer; no appearance/material claims added.
required_tests: uv run ruff check src tests
stop_conditions: Official Meta SAM 3.1 cannot be verified (ship placeholder + report; do not improvise).
Any change would touch pyproject.toml base dependencies or paths outside allowed_paths.
expected_outputs: Updated registry + sam31 backend (pinned or unavailable-until-pinned)
Verification note (official repo URL, revision, checkpoint hash or the documented absence)
